name: Andaman and Nicobar Islands (अंडमान और निकोबार द्वीप समूह)
summary: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands (अंडमान और निकोबार द्वीप समूह in Hindi) are a Bay of Bengal mixed common-law/indigenous-customary-law Indian Union Territory comprising the Andaman Islands group (300+ islands) and Nicobar Islands group (22 islands) — structurally distinctive globally as the only Indian jurisdiction with an uncontacted indigenous tribe (the Sentinelese of North Sentinel Island, who have remained isolated for ~60,000+ years and whose contact is explicitly prohibited under the Aboriginal Tribes (Andaman and Nicobar Islands) Regulation 1956 and 1991+ Eyes-and-Ears policy — the only modern formal state-level prohibition on contact with an uncontacted indigenous tribe), as the central jurisdiction of the most extensive Indian indigenous-tribe-protection framework operating five Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs): Great Andamanese, Onge, Jarawa, Sentinelese (all Negrito-origin), and Shompen (Mongoloid Nicobarese), and as the only Indian Union Territory with comprehensive Inner Line Permit-equivalent restricted-area framework via Restricted Area Permit (RAP) under the Foreigners (Protected Areas) Order 1958. The Andamans were Cellular Jail penal colony framework (Kālā Pānī, 'Black Water') under British colonial framework 1858-1942, were occupied by Imperial Japan 1942-1945, were proclaimed as the temporary Provisional Government of Free India by Subhas Chandra Bose's Azad Hind on 30 December 1943 (the only Indian territory under formal Azad Hind administration during WWII), and integrated into India following 1947. Andaman and Nicobar Islands comprise ~8,249 km² total area across ~500+ islands with a population of ~380,000 (~95% non-indigenous, ~5% indigenous tribal). Family-law framework operates under the federal Indian common-law framework (Hindu Marriage Act 1955, Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937, Guardians and Wards Act 1890) applied via Union Territory administrative framework with Aboriginal Tribes Regulation 1956 customary-law protection for the five PVTGs. The Calcutta High Court (Port Blair Circuit Bench) is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Supreme Court of India. Andaman and Nicobar Islands are silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. India is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Andaman and Nicobar Islands are governed by the Indian non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.citation: Aboriginal Tribes (Andaman and Nicobar Islands) Regulation 1956
statutory_framework.title: Aboriginal Tribes Regulation
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Federal Regulation of 1956 explicitly protecting indigenous tribes of Andaman and Nicobar Islands — including five PVTGs Great Andamanese, Onge, Jarawa, Sentinelese, Shompen.

statutory_framework.citation: Foreigners (Protected Areas) Order 1958 + Restricted Area Permit (RAP) framework
statutory_framework.title: Foreigners (Protected Areas) Order
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Federal Order establishing Restricted Area Permit framework for Andaman and Nicobar Islands — restricting foreigner access including specific prohibitions on entering tribal reserves.

statutory_framework.title: Sentinelese Eyes-and-Ears Policy
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Government Eyes-and-Ears policy framework of 1991 — formal prohibition on attempts to contact the Sentinelese tribe of North Sentinel Island.

statutory_framework.citation: Andaman and Nicobar Islands (Protection of Aboriginal Tribes) Amendment 2010
statutory_framework.title: Aboriginal Tribes Protection Amendment 2010
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Federal Amendment strengthening aboriginal-tribes-protection framework.

apex_courts.name: Calcutta High Court (Port Blair Circuit Bench)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of India
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

professional_regulators.name: Indian Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI)
professional_regulators.role: Indian Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

anonymisation_convention: Andaman and Nicobar Islands family-court decisions are anonymised per Indian court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Lt. Archibald Blair survey + Port Blair foundation
key_developments.description: Lt. Archibald Blair Royal Navy surveyed Andaman Islands 1789-1793 + founded Port Blair settlement framework — pivotal pre-modern British East India Company sovereignty foundation framework + foundation for subsequent 1858 Cellular Jail penal colony framework.

key_developments.title: Cellular Jail penal colony established
key_developments.description: British Cellular Jail (Kālā Pānī, 'Black Water') penal colony established 1858 following 1857 Indian Rebellion — beginning of Indian colonial penal colony framework that operated until 1942 + structurally distinctive globally only Indian Union Territory with British colonial penal-colony foundational framework + significant Indian nationalist context shaping subsequent Indian-independence-movement framework.

key_developments.title: Japanese occupation
key_developments.description: Japanese occupation of Andaman and Nicobar Islands March 1942-October 1945 — pivotal WWII Imperial Japan framework + significant context shaping subsequent 1943 Azad Hind administration framework + structurally distinctive globally only Indian territory under formal Japanese WWII occupation.

key_developments.title: Provisional Government of Free India
key_developments.description: Subhas Chandra Bose's Azad Hind proclaimed Provisional Government of Free India in Andaman and Nicobar Islands on 30 December 1943 + renamed Andaman as 'Shaheed' (Martyr) + Nicobar as 'Swaraj' (Self-rule) — structurally distinctive globally only Indian territory under formal Azad Hind administration during WWII + pivotal Indian-nationalist sovereignty framework foundation.

key_developments.title: Indian integration + Union Territory framework foundation
key_developments.description: Andaman and Nicobar Islands integrated into Indian Union 15 August 1947 + Part-D State 1950-1956 + Union Territory 1956+ — pivotal modern Indian sovereignty framework foundation + foundation for subsequent 1956 Aboriginal Tribes Regulation + Union Territory administrative framework.

key_developments.title: Aboriginal Tribes (A&N Islands) Regulation
key_developments.description: Indian Federal Regulation of 1956 explicitly protecting indigenous tribes of Andaman and Nicobar Islands — including five PVTGs (Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups): Great Andamanese, Onge, Jarawa, Sentinelese (all Negrito-origin), and Shompen (Mongoloid Nicobarese) + structurally distinctive globally most extensive Indian indigenous-tribe-protection framework.

key_developments.title: Sentinelese Eyes-and-Ears Policy
key_developments.description: Indian Government Eyes-and-Ears policy framework of 1991 — formal prohibition on attempts to contact the Sentinelese tribe of North Sentinel Island + structurally distinctive globally only modern formal state-level prohibition on contact with an uncontacted indigenous tribe + foundation for subsequent 2018 Chau incident framework.

key_developments.title: 2004 Indian Ocean earthquake-tsunami
key_developments.description: 2004 Indian Ocean earthquake-tsunami of 26 December 2004 devastated Andaman and Nicobar Islands particularly Nicobar Islands — over 7,000 deaths and substantial geomorphological transformation + Indira Point (southernmost Indian territory) submerged ~1.25m + significant context for subsequent A&N Islands disaster-management framework.

key_developments.title: John Allen Chau North Sentinel Island incident
key_developments.description: American missionary John Allen Chau killed by Sentinelese tribe on 17 November 2018 — reaffirming Indian Government non-contact policy framework + structurally distinctive globally only modern state-level reaffirmation of non-contact policy framework following missionary fatality + Indian Government declined to recover body or pursue prosecution to maintain non-contact framework.

key_developments.title: Lieutenant Governor + A&N Administration + Great Nicobar Holistic Development Project framework
key_developments.description: Lieutenant Governor and A&N Administration framework continuing through 2023 with institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Indian Federal family-law framework (Hindu Marriage Act 1955 + Guardians and Wards Act 1890) applied via Union Territory administrative framework + Aboriginal Tribes Regulation 1956 protection of PVTGs. Substantive Great Nicobar Holistic Development Project ~₹72,000 crore mega-project context affecting indigenous-rights and family-law-implementation framework. Substantively significant Bay-of-Bengal Indian-Union-Territory institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Continuing PVTGs framework + Great Nicobar mega-project context
key_developments.description: Continuing Andaman and Nicobar Islands PVTGs framework 2024 — continuing Aboriginal Tribes Regulation 1956 framework + continuing Sentinelese Eyes-and-Ears Policy + continuing five PVTGs protection + significant Great Nicobar Holistic Development Project context (2022+ Indian Government ~₹72,000 crore mega-project including international container transshipment port + greenfield airport + Vivekananda Shompen displacement concerns + ongoing environmental + indigenous-rights contestation framework).

structural_findings: Andaman and Nicobar Islands operate a mixed federal Indian common-law + indigenous-customary-law Union Territory framework — places A&N Islands in the Bay of Bengal Indian-Union-Territory cluster.
Only Indian jurisdiction with an uncontacted indigenous tribe (Sentinelese of North Sentinel Island) is structurally distinctive globally.
Only modern formal state-level prohibition on contact with an uncontacted indigenous tribe is structurally distinctive globally — Indian Eyes-and-Ears policy 1991 framework.

Central jurisdiction of most extensive Indian indigenous-tribe-protection framework operating five Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) is structurally distinctive globally — Great Andamanese, Onge, Jarawa, Sentinelese, Shompen.
Only Indian Union Territory with comprehensive Restricted Area Permit (RAP) framework under Foreigners (Protected Areas) Order 1958 is structurally distinctive.
Cellular Jail penal colony framework (Kālā Pānī, 'Black Water') 1858-1942 is structurally distinctive globally — only Indian Union Territory with British colonial penal-colony foundational framework.
1943 Provisional Government of Free India (Azad Hind) framework is structurally distinctive globally — only Indian territory under formal Azad Hind administration during WWII.
2018 John Allen Chau incident is structurally distinctive globally — only modern state-level reaffirmation of non-contact policy framework following missionary fatality.
editorial_notes: Andaman and Nicobar Islands jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — mixed federal Indian common-law/indigenous-customary-law Bay of Bengal Indian Union Territory (1789 Lt. Blair Port Blair foundation + 1858 Cellular Jail Kālā Pānī + 1942 Japanese occupation + 1943 Provisional Government of Free India Azad Hind + 1947 Indian integration + 1956 Aboriginal Tribes Regulation + five PVTGs + 1991 Sentinelese Eyes-and-Ears Policy + 2004 Indian Ocean earthquake-tsunami + 2018 Chau incident + 2024 Great Nicobar Holistic Development Project context). Only Indian jurisdiction with uncontacted indigenous tribe (Sentinelese) globally + only modern formal state-level prohibition on contact with uncontacted indigenous tribe + most extensive Indian indigenous-tribe-protection framework + only Indian Union Territory with British colonial penal-colony foundational framework + only Indian territory under formal Azad Hind administration during WWII.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Bay-of-Bengal + mixed Indian-common-law/indigenous-customary-law + Indian-Union-Territory cluster + Sentinelese-uncontacted-indigenous-tribe-globally-distinctive + Eyes-and-Ears-Policy-1991-non-contact + five-PVTGs-Great-Andamanese-Onge-Jarawa-Sentinelese-Shompen + Aboriginal-Tribes-Regulation-1956 + Foreigners-Protected-Areas-Order-1958-RAP + Cellular-Jail-Kālā-Pānī-penal-colony-1858-1942 + Provisional-Government-of-Free-India-Azad-Hind-1943 + 2018-Chau-incident + Indian-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
